dimension_results.raw_response:
ISSUE_1_VALIDATION_SCORE: 100 - The AI correctly identified that the receipt is missing the required ICP company details. According to the compliance rules, for telecommunications expenses in Germany with Global People as the ICP, invoices must show "Global People DE GmbH Taunusanlage 8, 60329 Frankfurt, Germany VAT ID: DE356366640". The rule explicitly states that "The Local Employers name and details should appear on invoices, not the workers." The receipt shows only the worker's name (Ingo Bruse) and not the required company details.

ISSUE_2_VALIDATION_SCORE: 100 - The AI correctly applied the rule that phone expenses are tax-free only up to 20 EUR per month in Germany. The total amount of 58.24 EUR exceeds this limit, so the AI properly identified that amounts above this limit are taxable and require gross-up. This directly follows the compliance rule stating "Phone/internet €20/month max tax-exempt, amounts above this limit are taxable."

ISSUE_3_VALIDATION_SCORE: 100 - The AI correctly identified the requirement for additional documentation to prove separate personal phone usage. According to the compliance rules for Global People in Germany, "Mobile phone usage can only be reimbursed tax-free if worker uses separate phone for personal use and can provide proof of this phone." The recommendation to provide proof of a separate personal phone is directly aligned with this rule.

CONFIDENCE: 100

ISSUES: None. All identified issues are correctly based on the compliance knowledge base.

SUMMARY: The AI response demonstrates perfect adherence to the compliance knowledge base for telecommunications expenses in Germany with Global People as the ICP. All three issues identified (missing company details on invoice, exceeding tax-exempt limit, and requirement for additional documentation) are directly supported by specific rules in the knowledge base. The recommendations provided are appropriate and aligned with the compliance requirements.
